74090.subject: AVALANCHE MAY 2026 RECAP
74090.body:
View this post on the web at https://www.team1.blog/p/avalanche-may-2026-recap

May proved Avalanche is where institutions move real capital at scale.
It also served as another strong reminder that blockchain is steadily moving beyond infrastructure narratives and into real-world business adoption.
CME launched AVAX futures with 24/7 trading. South Korea’s NHN KCP piloted stablecoin payments through PAYCO across more than 1,000 merchants and employees. Progmat announced plans to migrate over $2 billion in Japanese securities to its dedicated Avalanche L1 by June.
The momentum didn’t stop there. MapleStory Universe surpassed 150 million transactions, tokenized RWA value climbed to $1.34 billion, and AVAX became the 10th-largest stablecoin network. Team1 also officially launched LatAm as a unified chapter spanning 10 countries.
Here’s the full breakdown of everything that went down in May.
Want to keep up with the latest in the Avalanche ecosystem? Subscribe to the Team1 blog and get our updates in your inbox weekly.
CME Launches AVAX Futures with 24/7 Trading:
CME Group made AVAX and SUI futures available for trading on May 2nd, with expanded 24/7 crypto futures and options starting May 29th, opening institutional derivatives access. [Launch announcement [ https://substack.com/redirect/c599a802-85e2-49b7-825a-427f498cfe32?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ]]
AVAX Becomes 10th Largest Stablecoin Network:
AVAX stablecoin supply jumped 16.35% in one week to $1.66B, becoming the 10th largest stablecoin network with BlackRock’s BUIDL among the largest assets. [Network ranking [ https://substack.com/redirect/6b21bfab-0289-4325-98e4-822d7b16a924?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ]]
RWA Market Cap Reaches New All-Time High:
Tokenized RWA value reached $1.34B ATH, with distributed RWAs hitting new records as assets use Avalanche as distribution layer. [RWA milestone [ https://substack.com/redirect/afb1e196-a1ea-498a-8eb9-72d529f6984b?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ]]
May saw Team1 reach unprecedented scale with LatAm officially launching and regional chapters coordinating at institutional and grassroots levels worldwide.
Team1 LatAm Officially Launches as Unified Chapter:
Team1 LatAm became official after building momentum across 10 countries with 100+ prior events, 11,000+ Luma subscribers, 2,000+ developers and builders engaged, and 20 university collaborations. Team1 LatAm partnered with NVIDIA to connect builders with startup programs, cloud credits, and venture capital opportunities. [LatAm launch [ https://substack.com/redirect/0955f869-3cd4-410a-9da4-408692a0405b?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ]]
Team1 Regional Chapters Expand Globally:
In the UK Team1 hosted its inaugural London Connect [ https://substack.com/redirect/44aed39f-c0d7-42da-9804-8c2a1e3eac5b?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy

74088.subject: The Complete Guide to Claude Dynamic Workflows 🤖
74088.body:
View this post on the web at https://linas.substack.com/p/claude-code-dynamic-workflows-guide

👋 Hey, Linas here! Every day, I break down 3 stories shaping the future of FinTech & Artificial Intelligence - plus the money movements and trends worth tracking. First time here? 388k+ FinTech and AI leaders get this daily. Join them:
On May 28, 2026, Anthropic shipped arguably the most important upgrade to Claude Code [ https://substack.com/redirect/4e1566e7-40ca-4a0a-80b8-ee4476b6f974?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] since its launch.
It was not a new model, and not a faster API. It was something more fundamental: the ability for Claude to write its own orchestration program on the fly, spin up a coordinated fleet of AI agents, and hand back a single verified result - all from one prompt.
The feature is called Dynamic Workflows [ https://substack.com/redirect/0e24ab16-9110-4237-9261-707bc0b5e8fb?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ]. And most early adopters are using it wrong.
Some burned through millions of tokens before learning the basics. Others dismissed it as a tool for large-scale code migrations and missed the fact that it works equally well for hiring decisions, investor due diligence, content pipelines, security audits, and business plan stress-testing. A handful of teams have already used it to compress months of engineering work into days - most visibly, a 750,000-line codebase rewrite completed in just 11 days 😳
Until now, understanding the full picture meant piecing together fragments across Anthropic’s docs, the original blog post by the feature’s author, community war stories on Reddit and Hacker News, expert practitioner breakdowns, and insights shared by the Claude Code team internally.
This is all of that synthesized into one guide - covering not just what Dynamic Workflows are, but why they were built, how the orchestration works under the hood, which of the six patterns to use for which task, how to avoid the token-cost traps that have blindsided entire teams, and what risks Anthropic has not put front-and-center in the docs.
Whether you write code or not, whether you are a solo founder or leading a 200-person engineering team, whether you are evaluating AI tooling for investment or trying to 10x your own output - this guide will tell you exactly what Dynamic Workflows can do for you, and exactly when they will burn you if you are not careful.
What Are Dynamic Workflows?...

Unsubscribe https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9saW5hcy5zdWJzdGFjay5jb20vYWN0aW9uL2Rpc2FibGVfZW1haWw_

74087.subject: Nvidia's best model is a free download
74087.body:
----------
######                      [Sign Up](http://utm.genai.works/r/gg2ubr5) | [Collaborate with us](https://6hhfxazcbpc.typeform.com/to/uXIl0IFH?utm_source=atlas&utm_medium=newsletter&utm_campaign=general_2026_05_11&utm_content=banner_post)


--------------------
View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8776fa6e-e2d0-4aec-9648-1bed6de3a659/GenAI_Daily_Banner.jpg?t=1778687445)
Caption: 


----------

----------
**Welcome back!** Nvidia put a frontier model on the internet for free, training data and recipes included. OpenAI rebuilt ChatGPT's memory so it updates itself while you're away, and Apple is reportedly renting Nvidia chips to run the Google model behind the Siri it unveils Monday. Netflix, meanwhile, is testing a voice interface that finds you something to watch.

**In today's Generative AI Newsletter:**

* **Nvidia:** Why give away a frontier model when you could charge for it?

* **ChatGPT memory:** What does ChatGPT do with your memories between conversations?

* **Apple:** How much of the new Siri is actually Apple's?

* **Netflix:** Why is Netflix teaching its app to listen?


----------

----------
## [Nvidia's best model is a free download](https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/nvidia-nemotron-3-ultra-powers-faster-more-efficient-reasoning-for-long-running-agents/)

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/fc8f661d-a34a-4fdf-af36-c53a1826bcd8/ChatGPT_Image_Jun_5__2026__08_57_23_AM.png?t=1780639084)
Caption: 

Nvidia shipped Nemotron 3 Ultra on Thursday, the strongest model it has ever released, and gave the whole thing away. Weights, training data and the recipes it used to build it. Anyone can download it, fine-tune it and sell whatever they build on top.

It's built for the expensive part of AI agents. An agent working a long task spends most of its time, and your money, deciding what to do between tool calls. Nemotron mixes Mamba layers, which skim long history cheaply, with transformer layers for exact recall, so each step costs less to think through.

* **The pitch:** 5x the throughput of comparable open models and up to 30% cheaper on long agent tasks, by Nvidia's own benchmarks.

* **The scores:** 65 to 70.4% on SWE-bench Verified, a test built from real GitHub bug fixes, with the score holding across five different agent frameworks.

* **The giveaway:** 212 billion fresh training tokens, 10 million fine-tuning samples and the full training recipes, under OpenMDW, the Linux Foundation's license written for open models.

* **Where to try it:** Live on OpenRouter, Perplexity for Pro subscribers and [build.nvidia.com](https://build.nvidia.com), with weights on Hugging Face.

What actually changed is who gets to run a model like this. Plenty of teams can't send their data to an outside API, and the open models wo

CMC Market Pulse:
The Essential Weekly Crypto Briefing

Welcome to this week’s CMC Market Pulse newsletter!

CMC Market Pulse delivers actionable market insights, giving you
all the key narratives and developments you need in under 5
minutes.

Let's dive into this week.

Market Overview 📈
-----------------

Bitcoin (BTC) crashed further this week, dropping
15.36%,
while Ethereum (ETH) continued its underperformance, falling a
whopping
16.95%
in the same week. Unsurprisingly, total crypto market cap fell
hard as well, dropping
2.86%
to
$2.14T,
down from $2.47T from the previous week.

Weekly liquidations saw a huge spike over this week on the
increased downward volatility, taking out almost $3.5B across
Tuesday to Thursday. Funding rates collapsed together with the
move, as the bearish sentiment only continues to exacerbate.
Funding rates for several crypto majors including ETH and SOL are
now in negative territory.

* Michael Saylor’s Strategy sells 32 BTC for $2.5M in their
latest 8-K filing, marking their first BTC sale since 2022.
* ETH treasury company, BitMine files for a preferred stock
offering, with a proposed yield of 9.5%, mimicking Strategy’s
playbook.
* The U.S. CFTC rules to enable the listing of perpetual futures
by Designated Contract Markets (DCMs).
Despite closing weaker, equities crack a new high this week
while crypto spirals downwards, continuing the decoupling from
the trad
